description: Copy makes a bold, collective statement that invites the audience to identify with a shared Saudi identity, regional pride, or community belonging. The brand positions itself as a fellow member of that community — not as an outside vendor. Often uses 'we', 'our', 'together', 'you and us'.
structural_recipe: Short declarative statement (1-2 lines) making a pride claim: 'هذه بلادنا', 'نحن أهل الكرم', 'من هنا وإلى هنا'. Followed by brand connection that feels like a natural extension of that pride. Tone: confident, warm, proud — never boastful. No product price or mechanic in this frame.
why_it_works: Creates instant tribal alignment. Saudi audiences respond to brands that speak as community members, not marketers. When the brand says 'نحن' (we) and it lands authentically, it earns loyalty beyond any single transaction.
transplantation_caveats: International brands using 'we' in Saudi collective identity statements read as appropriation — only works for genuine Saudi-origin brands.
Regional pride statements (Najdi, Hejazi) only work if the brand actually has that heritage.
Avoid over-claiming — 'we are Saudi' from a franchise brand is immediately rejected.
Keep statements broad enough to be inclusive but specific enough to feel real.

provenance.source: corpus_synthesis_474_obs — 12 observed uses in f_and_b and retail sectors
